scan_table_check.description: New capabilities require new guardrails on how to use them. The platform's AI Agent data model allows the re-use of AI Agent Tools across multiple AI Agents. The table sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m connect the actual tool record with AI Agents as a many-to-many relationship. Based on real world project experience re-using tools across multiple agents is however not supporting maintainability and test-ability of a system. Testing AI Agents in itself is a new discipline and various ideas and patterns are emerging to validate the results of AI Agents. Due to the non-deterministic nature and the influence of the input data, the prompt itself and the information fed into the AI Agent through Tools, this easily becomes a three body problem (i.e. hard if not even impossible to solve). To reduce the level of complexity and increase de-coupling, the re-use of Tools across multiple AI Agents should be avoided - as a change in one Tool would directly impact the output of multiple AI Agents. This guidance is implemented by ensuring that a Tool is only wired to one Agent through a single "Agent Tool" m2m record.
scan_table_check.name: CodeSanity - AI Agent Tool Structure
scan_table_check.resolution_details: If a Tool is re-used across multiple AI Agents, the Tool must be copied and hence de-coupled from any other AI Agent.
scan_table_check.short_description: AI Agent Tools must be used by one AI Agent only
